title: salix cordata subsp. rigida
intro: Salix cordata subsp. rigida, also known as the stiff-leaved willow, is a subspecies of the Salix cordata willow plant, which is a type of deciduous shrub or small tree commonly found in wetlands and riparian areas. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, derived from its bark which contains salicin, a compound similar to aspirin. The health benefits of Salix cordata subsp. rigida include relief from headaches, fever reduction, and relief from rheumatic and arthritic pain, as well as being used to treat skin irritations and wounds. Herbal preparations of this plant include teas, infusions, and salves made from the bark, which can be used internally and externally to harness its medicinal properties.
